mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and improves equity valuation multiples, so a sustained easing trend should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative or below unconditional baseline in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS has declined for at least 3 consecutive monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL inflation pressure, real policy becomes restrictive and financial conditions tighten, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the spread versus unconditional returns is not statistically negative over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, so an upward unemployment shock should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than half of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in HOUST and RSAFS indicates resilient housing turnover and retail demand, which supports earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly gains in HOUST and RSAFS fail to coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and supply-side momentum, which should support cyclical earnings and generate positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not higher following positive INDPRO momentum than following flat or negative momentum, or if the sign consistency is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 growth after an expansion peak signals demand deceleration that compresses revenue growth expectations, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth quarters are not followed by below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once revisions stabilize.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST identifies weakening consumption and housing demand, and that joint deterioration should lead to negative S&P 500 returns within one month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both fall month over month
failure_condition: Reject if joint monthly declines in RSAFS and HOUST are not associated with negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if either series alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, a CPIAUCSL inflation rise combined with a high FEDFUNDS rate implies real-rate pressure and multiple compression, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-and-rate condition does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the signal is positive in sustained tightening episodes.
